candidates.name: Tim Walz
candidates.notes: Governor since 2019. 2024 Democratic VP nominee. Former high school teacher and football coach. Former House member (2007-2019) from rural MN district where he had NRA A rating — now F as governor. Signed abortion protection, trans refuge, free school meals, paid family leave, marijuana legalization. Signed gun control measures. Previously more moderate on guns and immigration as House member. Lutheran who supports religious liberty broadly but not religious exemptions from anti-discrimination laws.

profile.education: Chadron State College (BS); Minnesota State University, Mankato (MS, Educational Leadership)
profile.background: High school teacher and football coach; Army National Guard veteran (24 years); U.S. Representative (2007-2019); became 40th governor in 2019; 2024 Democratic VP nominee.

candidates.name: Amy Klobuchar
candidates.notes: Senator since 2007. 2020 presidential candidate. Heritage Action 0% current, 4% lifetime. NRA F rated. Former Hennepin County Attorney. Pro-choice. Supports assault weapons ban and universal background checks. Supports DREAM Act. Supports Equality Act. Known as pragmatic moderate Democrat but votes consistently liberal on social issues. Focuses on antitrust and tech regulation.

profile.education: Yale University (BA Political Science), University of Chicago Law School (JD)
profile.background: Former Hennepin County Attorney. Attorney at Dorsey & Whitney. 2020 presidential candidate. In Senate since 2007. Known for bipartisan legislation and antitrust work.

candidates.name: Tina Smith
candidates.notes: Senator since 2018 (appointed to replace Al Franken, elected 2018 and 2020). Heritage Action 0% current, 4% lifetime. NRA F rated. Former MN Lt. Governor under Mark Dayton. Former Planned Parenthood VP. Pro-choice champion. Supports assault weapons ban. Supports DREAM Act. Previously worked at General Mills.

profile.education: Stanford University (BA Political Science), Dartmouth Tuck School of Business (MBA)
profile.background: Former Minnesota Lieutenant Governor and Planned Parenthood executive. Former chief of staff to Governor Dayton. Business strategy consultant. Appointed to Senate in 2018.

candidates.name: Bobby Joe Champion
candidates.notes: President of the Minnesota Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Minnesota_Senate.
